behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit, then escalate to Integration because this failure appears at a module handoff: diff parser/output must be consumed correctly by the prompt/input builder, and mis-wiring can include unchanged lines even if each unit works alone. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess is required; fixture diffs and in-process adapters are enough, so not System. (b) correctness here is about input scoping, not model output quality, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only requires input scoping ('scoped to the lines that changed'), not real external infra or model-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules for diff ingestion + changed-line extraction + review-input assembly using representative patch fixtures; assert assembled review payload contains only changed hunks/lines and excludes unchanged file content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration: mapping logic crosses interfaces (finding metadata -> API payload fields). Integration->System: real PR diff-position semantics are enforced by the external platform; mocked clients often accept invalid line/side/commit combinations, hiding real failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about positional attachment in PR diff view, not LLM output quality or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real test PR on the hosting platform, post inline comments from computed positions, then fetch PR review comments and verify each lands on the intended file/hunk/line in the actual diff context.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration: detection uses multiple modules (PR content preparation + detector invocation + result parsing). Integration->System: real external call path is needed to exercise true runtime behavior of the detector service/model endpoint. System->Agentic: pass/fail depends on model output quality/non-determinism (whether a real bug is actually recognized), which stubs would mask. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior requires one detection outcome ('identifies at least one real defect'), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real PR fixture with a seeded known bug and call the real model-backed reviewer repeatedly under controlled prompt/settings; assert at least one returned finding correctly flags the known defect category/location.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration: this is a cross-module consistency invariant between inline-comment posting results and summary composer output; failures occur when state handoff is wrong. Integration-brake: (a) real OS/network/subprocess is not inherently required to catch this invariant; a fake comment gateway that returns success/failure IDs can reproduce it, so not System. (b) no dependence on model quality; this checks correspondence, not finding quality, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text focuses correspondence invariant ('summary corresponds to an inline comment') rather than platform-specific transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with real local modules and a controllable in-process comment service fake that records successful inline posts; assert summary entries are generated only from successfully posted inline comment records and each summary item links to a recorded inline comment.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration: this is orchestration across multiple modules (diff reader, inline commenter, summary poster). Integration->System: behavior explicitly requires a real pull request end-to-end, so real external API behavior/auth/rate/response handling must be exercised. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because the behavior text is completion-oriented ('full sequence ... completes correctly') and does not require judging model output quality; rejected Workflow because no concrete requirement for multiple agentic steps in sequence is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the automation against a real sandbox PR and verify unattended completion: diff retrieved, inline comments successfully created, summary comment successfully created, and run exits with success while recording artifacts/IDs for each step.
